description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Astoria Park is home to the oldest and largest pool in New York City. The park features views of Manhattan and a newly renovated track funded by the Anchor Parks initiative.

Canarsie Park serves the neighborhoods of Canarsie and nearby Flatlands, Mill Basin, Marine Park, and Bergen Beach. Home to baseball fields and cricket pitches, it's bordered by the Belt Parkway and Paerdegat Basin.

It's home to over 150 species of birds and to the Ridgewood Reservoir.

name: NYRR Open Run: Inwood Hill Park New Runner Clinic
description: NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic is your start to a healthier lifestyle. With guidance and support from NYRR coaches and local run club members, you'll learn everything you need to take on your first mile. NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Evidence of Inwood Hill Park's formation exists as caves, valleys, and ridges left as the result of shifting glaciers. Evidence of its uninhabited state afterward remains as its forest and salt marsh (the last natural one in Manhattan), and evidence of its use by Native Americans continues to be discovered. Most of the park was largely untouched by wars and development. The park features a hiking trail and the Hudson River Bike Trail, and is one of the best places in NYC to spot a bald eagle. It also includes athletic fields, playgrounds, dog run...

address: Seaman Avenue and Isham Street Entrance in Inwood Hill Park

name: NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic: St. Marys Park
description: NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic is your start to a healthier lifestyle. With guidance and support from NYRR coaches and local run club members, you'll learn everything you need to take on your first mile. NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. St. Mary's Park offers a wonderful respite from the hustle and bustle of urban life. Located in the Mott Haven neighborhood in the South Bronx, the park features rolling hills, expanses of green grass, and grand old trees. The site was once part of the estate of Jonas Bronck (1600-43), for whom the Bronx is named. It is one of the largest parks in the area with a pool, playground, basketball courts, an indoor recreation center, picnic seating areas, and more. In the winter, the hills are perfect for sledding. St. Mary's Park received $30 million from N...

name: NYRR Open Run: Shore Road Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Shore Road Park, located in Bay Ridge, Brooklyn, is one of our longer Open Run courses. The park serves residents of Bay Ridge, Sunset Park, Dyker Heights, Fort Hamilton, and other surrounding neighborhoods. The course is three loops alongside Shore Road with stunning views of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge.

name: NYRR Open Run: Soundview Park
description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Called the \"Gateway to the Bronx River,\" 205-acre Soundview Park is located where the Bronx River opens into the East River. This urban green space is filled with grassy baseball and soccer fields, a cricket pitch, basketball courts, a running track, walking\/biking paths, picnic fields, and more! With its extensive views of the water, Soundview Park celebrates the beauty of both the Bronx and East Rivers. The back part of our Open Run course has beautiful views of the Bronx River waterfront for participants to enjoy!

name: Summer Streets: Queens
description:
Vernon Boulevard, between 44 Drive and 30th Road, will be open for people to play, walk, and bike from 9am to 5pm. 
 Our city's usual traffic noise and honking horns will be replaced with open streets teeming with walkers, bikers, runners, and performers. Discover a wealth of free cultural programs, performances, fitness classes, interactive art, giveaways, and much more as you experience the city in this whole new way. 
 This year's event occurs on July 25 in Queens and Staten Island, August 1, 8, and 15 in Manhattan, and August 22 in Brooklyn and the Bronx. 
 Visit nyc.gov/summerstreets for location details and programming updates. 
 Summers Streets is a rain or shine event.

name: City of Water Day at Kaiser Park
description: Join the Stewardship Team in celebrating City of Water Day at Kaiser Park! This is a large community event where many organizations will be tabling and other activities will be available. We will be leading a coastal cleanup as well as a seining demo. Come dressed in sturdy boots or shoes, long pants, and clothing that can get dirty. Space is limited and registration is required. Volunteers under 18 must be accompanied by a chaperone. To sign up a group of 5 or more volunteers, please reach out to stewardship@parks.nyc.gov.
address: Kaiser Park Beach in Kaiser Park

name: City of Water Day at Sunset Cove Park
description: This City of Water Day, volunteer with the Stewardship team to protect coastal areas of Sunset Cove Park! Attendees will help us remove debris that has washed ashore, contributing to the ongoing resilience of Sunset Cove's coastline. Please come dressed in sturdy boots or closed-shoes, long pants and clothing that can get dirty. Please DO NOT wear Crocs, sandals or shorts. Space is limited and registration is required. Volunteers under 18 must be accompanied by a chaperone. To sign up a group of 10 or more volunteers, please reach out to stewardship@parks.nyc.gov.
address: Sunset Cove

name: City of Water Day: Soundview Park Guided Walk
description: Join NAC staff for an exploration of Soundview Park's Trails. We'll discuss plant identification and maritime woodlands. The Natural Areas Conservancy will host a guided walk on the trails at Soundview Park as part of City of Water Day in order to explore the trail and learn about our local ecosystem. Attendees will learn about plant id, maritime woodlands, and aquatic plants If you are attending, please fill out this volunteer waiver . All ages are welcome. Attendees under 18 will need to be accompanied by an adult. What you should bring: A water bottleWeather-appropriate clothingSturdy, closed-toe shoesInsect repellentLong pants and tall socks recommendedIf you have any questions about this event or would like to learn more about the Natural Areas Conservancy, please visit naturalareasnyc.org, @naturalareasnyc on Ins...
address: Soundview Park

name: City of Water Day with Friends of Inwood Hill Park
description: Now in its 19th year, City of Water Day is a region-wide day organized by Waterfront Alliance and New York-New Jersey Harbor & Estuary Program (HEP), to champion a climate-resilient New York and New Jersey harbor. Friends of Inwood Hill Park is joining the Waterfront Alliance for another year to bring waterside programming to Inwood Hill Park alongside some of our favorite partner organizations, including the Urban Park Rangers, BioBus, the American Museum of Natural History, Riverkeeper, and more! We will meet in front of the Inwood Hill Nature Center for a full day of activities, including nature walks, trash cleanups, and more, to celebrate the water that connects our neighborhood and Inwood Hill Park to the larger Hudson River community and beyond.
address: Inwood Hill Nature Center in Inwood Hill Park

name: Community Recycling Event - Soundview
description:
DSNY holds pop-up e-waste and textile recycling events in every Community Board district in New York City. 
 E-waste items collected at these events include: household and office electronics (computers, laptops, keyboards, printers), smaller entertainment electronics (video game consoles, cell phones, MP3 players), and TVs, VCRs, DVDs, DVRs. 
 Textile items include: clothing, shoes, hats, bags, gloves, towels, curtains, bedding, and all clean, dry, unsoiled fabrics. 
 Unacceptable items: commercial waste, air conditioners, household appliances, batteries, lightbulbs.
address: 1000 Rosedale Avenue

name: Dance Fitness- Upbeat Pop!
description: A 45-minute dance fitness class to your favorite upbeat pop songs! Come prepared to shake, roll, grapevine, and clap. We will use this time to cultivate joy and silliness while getting a great workout and reaping the benefits of cardiovascular fitness. All levels are welcome and encouraged. Whether you want to learn some new choreography or need a space to step-touch and sing, this class is for you!

A NYC Parks staff member leads four to seven hours of free activities in NYC's playgrounds, including organized sports, games, fitness demos, board games, and more! Kids in Motion is a free, drop-in program without registration. Children can head to the playground during the listed hours of operation to participate. Occasionally a Kids in Motion program may be cancelled due to harsh weather or other conditions. Please note: We recommend that guardians accompany children participating in this program. Kids in Motion is not a childcare program and NYC parks is not responsible for the well-being of attendees.

name: Moves with Limón Dance at Bryant Park
description: Move to live accompaniment and experience the joy of dance with dancers from the Limón. Open to all levels and ages - no experience required! Bring friends, family, water on hot days, and enjoy dancing on the grass. Class meets on the northeast corner of the lawn.
address: Bryant Park Lawn in Bryant Park

name: NYC Public Beaches
description:
Parks maintains 14 miles of beaches, which open Memorial Day weekend. During beach season, lifeguards are on duty daily, from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. Swimming is prohibited when lifeguards are not on duty and in closed sections. Closed sections are marked with signs and/or red flags. 
 
 Sign up for Notify NYC and opt-in to "beach notifications" to receive real-time opening and closing notifications. 
 Photo credit: Benny Polatseck/Mayoral Photography Office
address: NYC Public Beaches Citywide

name: Queens Recreation: Summer Sports Experience
description: The Summer Sports Experience season is in full swing! Join us for free sports instruction and competition. Activities include sports, games, demonstrations, and more.
address: Play Area

name: Rise&Restore Circuit Training
description: This circuit training class combines bodyweight resistance training and conditioning to provide you with a dynamic full body workout. Whether you are a beginner or looking for a challenge; this class for all fitness levels and modifications are always provided. Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups, get your heart pumping, and connect with people in your community!
location_name: Dance Room in Highbridge Recreation Center
address: Dance Room in Highbridge Recreation Center

name: Strong For Life Circuit Training
description: A welcoming exercise class focused on helping participants feel stronger, more mobile, and more energized in daily life. Class includes low-impact strength and endurance exercises, mobility training, functional fitness and balance work. Modifications will be provided for all levels of fitness.
address: BPL- Macon Library

name: Summer Sports Experience: St. Mary's Park \"Game of Zones Girls Basketball Clinic\"
description: If you love basketball and want to improve your skills, join game of zones at St. Mary's Park ! Whether you are a beginner or experienced player who wants to come and improve their skills and play, do it with us! Saturdays | 10:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m. | 8 - 11 yrs. Saturdays | 11:30 a.m. - 2:00 p.m. | 12 - 14 yrs.
address: Basketball Courts in St. Mary's Park

name: Yoga in the Park
description: Stretch, breathe, and find your inner peace at Randall's Island Park's free outdoor yoga series. Guided by a beloved, local yoga instructor, participants will flow through a calming and grounding sequence against the backdrop of the uptown skyline. All levels and abilities are welcome. Bring a friend, family member or two, or your yoga-loving pet! No registration required-just show up ready to move! We'll have water and a limited number of mats available to borrow, so please bring your own if possible or show up early to claim one. Our free yoga classes run weekly on Tuesdays from 6:30 - 7:30 pm from May 5 - September 1, and Saturdays from 10:00 - 11:00 am, July 11 - August 29. On Saturdays, stay after and join our dance class! Please check Instagram for the most updated schedule. Location: East River Picnic ...
address: East River Picnic Area in Randall's Island Park

name: LaBlast® Fitness
description: This dance fitness program is based on ballroom dances, including those seen on Dancing with the Stars. This workout in disguise blends dance patterns, fitness, and music to create a low impact workout suitable for all levels.
address: Brooklyn Public Library - New Utrecht

name: STRONG Nation®
description: STRONG Nation® combines body weight, muscle conditioning, cardio and plyometric training moves synced to original music that has been specifically designed to match every single move. Every squat, every lunge, every burpee is driven by the music, helping you make it to that last rep, and maybe even five more. *Participants must bring a lock and lock up all personal belongings.
location_name: Rock Climbing Wall Room in Gertrude Ederle Recreation Center
address: Rock Climbing Wall Room in Gertrude Ederle Recreation Center

name: Summer Sport Experience: Basketball: Skills and Drills
description: Summer Sports Experience is an instructional youth program that teaches sports skills. Our knowledgeable NYC Parks staff train kids in sports such as basketball, ball hockey, kickball, flag football, pickleball, soccer, team handball, track and field, and volleyball. This program covers basic and more advanced techniques. Summer Sports Experience is a drop-in program recommended for children ages 8-14. Participants can stop by for one or more sessions, but kids who stay for the whole program will get the most benefit from it. Occasionally a Summer Sports Experience program may be cancelled due to harsh weather or other conditions. Please note: We recommend that guardians accompany children participating in this program. Summer Sports Experience is not a childcare program, and NYC Parks is not responsible for the well-being of attendees. For more information, please visit nyc.gov\/parks. Contact accessibility@parks.nyc.gov or (212) 360-1430 for m...
address: Play Area in Brigadier General Charles Young Playground

name: Cardio Punch
description: Cardio Punch is a total body, non-contact workout fit for all levels. Punch, hook, and kick to motivating, high-energy music that progress into heart-pumping combinations.
address: Boardwalk at Beach 60th Street in Rockaway Beach and Boardwalk

name: City of Water Day: Boardwalk Tidal Tales
description: Celebrate City of Water Day with our experienced Urban Park Rangers. Let's talk about intersections between land and shore at the most culturally active settlement at the mouth of the New York Harbor!
address: Poseidon Playground

name: City of Water Day: Saltwater Fishing
description: Want to go fishing but don't know how or where to start? Join our Urban Park Rangers for free lessons! Learn how to properly use a fishing rod. Our experienced Rangers teach the ethics of fishing and the ecology of our waterways. All equipment will be provided. Recommended for ages 8 and older.
address: Entrance-West Street and Greenpoint Avenue in WNYC Transmitter Park

name: City of Water Day: Silver Lake
description: Celebrate City of Water Day with the Urban Park Rangers. Learn how Silver Lake supports local wildlife and connects to New York City's larger water system.
location_name: Forest Avenue and Silver Lake Park Road
address: Forest Avenue and Silver Lake Park Road in Silver Lake Park

name: Community Work Day
description: Roll up your sleeves and get your hands dirty at an upcoming volunteer day! Stop by the Wyckoff Urban Farm on the fourth Saturday of every month from April-October between 11AM-1PM to to volunteer alongside our farm staff to care for our space and participate in special work projects. Learn about what we're growing, get to know our farmers, and get your hands dirty with farm projects! Tickets are not required to attend, but it's always helpful to RSVP so we can plan projects accordingly. THINGS TO KNOW: This event is family-friendly (ages 10+). We welcome individuals and families with kids ages 10 and over but children must be accompanied by an adult for the duration of their stay.Dress for the mess! Make sure to wear clothes that you don't mind getting messy in and wear close toed shoes to protect your feet.Come Prepared: We will provide all the tools and protective gear for your projects but coming prepared with a hat, long sleeved shirt, sunscreen, and bug spray...
address: Wyckoff Farmhouse Museum in Fidler-Wyckoff House Park

name: Dance Fitness

This class is appropriate for all fitness levels.

description: Bring your energy and get ready to move at Merengue in the Park! Set against the backdrop of the waterfront and summer breeze, this lively class focuses on rhythm, connection, and fun. Participants will learn easy-to-follow steps and enjoy the fast-paced spirit of Merengue with an enthusiastic instructor leading the way. Perfect for all skill levels, this is a great opportunity to try something new or refine your moves. Come early for our 10 AM yoga class to warm up beforehand! description: Terra cotta sculpture garden featuring antique architectural ornaments from Tottenville's Atlantic Terra Cotta Company (west side lawn of Biddle House). NYC Parks and the Tottenville Historical Society invite you to visit a unique collection of terracotta artifacts that chronicle turn-of-the-twentieth-century architectural styles common in New York City and across the United States.

description: Phoenix Community Garden's Farm Stand is open for the season, every Saturday rain or shine until mid-November. Each bag contains local farm fresh fruit, salad greens, the best seasonal vegetables, and aromatics and herbs for flavor. There is sliding scale pricing. Cards, tap to pay and EBT are accepted at the register. Learn more and pre-order on the garden's website here . Pick up at Phoenix Community Garden, 24 Somers Street, between 11:00 a.m. and 3:00 p.m.
address: Phoenix Community Garden

name: Salt Marsh Saturday: City of Water Day Seining in the Harlem River
description: Have you ever explored the Harlem River? Randall's Island Park Alliance will host a free seining program on City of Water Day! Grab a pair of waders and help drag a seining net along the shore of the Harlem River to discover the amazing biodiversity in our waterways. Learn how to identify fish, crabs, and jellyfish. All equipment is provided, but wear clothes you don't mind getting wet! You must be 12 years or older to seine, but all are welcome to participate in our shoreline activities. Now in its 19th year, City of Water Day - taking place this year on Saturday, July 25 - is a region-wide day organized by Waterfront Alliance and New York-New Jersey Harbor & Estuary Program (HEP) , to champion a climate-resilient New York and New Jersey harbor. From 11 AM - 2 PM, our Natural Areas staff will...
address: Water's Edge Garden in Randall's Island Park

Whether you are a beginner who wants to learn proper form or know your way around the gym and looking for a challenge- this class is for you! Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups and will get your heart pumping.

name: Bronx River City of Water Day Celebration
description: Join the Bronx River Alliance on Saturday July 25, from 12-4pm at Starlight Park, to celebrate City of Water Day! We're coming together to celebrate our waterfronts and help build resilient, connected communities. There are so many ways to enjoy this free family-friendly event! Activities include: Learn about the wildlife of the Bronx River on a Guided Nature WalkHydration Station with teas foraged from the Bronx River FoodwayInteractive Community DrummingTea and Healing Sound BathFresh Produce Distribution Indigenous Art Workshop with the North American Indigenous Center of New York Canoe-Painting Art ProjectWeaving WorkshopCanoeing River Cleanup ( registration required )And more!
address: Bronx River House in Starlight Park

name: Celebrate Shorebirds
description: Love the beach? So do nesting shorebirds, like the piping plover. These tiny shorebirds fly over 700 miles to nest at Rockaway Beach every year. Join NYC Park's Piping Plover Team and other community partners for a day of educational activities and crafts to help us better understand how we can all share the beach and support a healthy living environment. Event is for all ages, with activities geared to younger audiences.
address: Rockaway Beach and Boardwalk - Boardwalk at Beach 60th Street

name: Chair Yoga
description: Connect with yourself in Chair Yoga, a class to be enjoyed by all- but designed for those who prefer to be seated. Experience a fun and light-hearted class that provides a seated flow. The goal is to raise your heart rate, challenge all muscle groups and improve balance while remaining seated with minimal impact on joints. Enjoy breath work to ensure you leave class feeling challenged and balanced!
location_name: Ballroom in Fort Hamilton Senior Recreation Center
address: Ballroom in Fort Hamilton Senior Recreation Center

name: Dance Fitness
description: Dance Fitness is a creative workout that fuses dance and aerobics. The goal is to improve cardio fitness while having fun and getting a full body workout! This class is taught to music with easy-to-follow steps that will surely get your heart pumping!
address: Brooklyn Public Library - Bay Ridge

name: Indigenous Art Workshop with NAIC-NY
description: Join the the North American Indigenous Center of New York and the Bronx River Alliance for an Indigenous Art Workshop at Starlight Park! This family and kid-friendly event will offer participants an opportunity to learn traditional indigenous crafts, and gain exposure to contemporary indigenous cultures. This activity is first come, first served. This project is made possible with funds from the Statewide Community Regrants Program, a regrant program of the New York State Council on the Arts with support of the Office of the Governor and the New York State Legislature and administered by the Bronx Council on the Arts.
address: Bronx River House in Starlight Park

name: One Book One Bronx book club
description: Come join the garden group at Bronx River Community Garden for a One Book One Bronx event! This is a weekly community space that sparks powerful conversations and celebrates diverse voices. Saturdays, May 23-August 8, registration required.

description: The Summer Sports Experience programs participants receive age-appropriate instruction through organized clinics, skill stations, drills, conditioning, and live game situations led by NYC Parks Staff.

address: Multi-Use Room B in Faber Park Recreation Center

name: Tea and Meditation with Tia Colon
description: Join us on The Bronx River with Meditation Facilitator Mattia \"Tia\" Colón MSW, in this immersive soundbath meditation + herbal tea tasting which invites participants (kids, caregivers, elders, everyone!) into a sacred space of calmness and tranquility. Through the soothing sounds and gentle vibrations of Tibetan meditation instruments, we'll open pathways for healing, stress relief and focus. After the meditation, we'll gather together for an herbal tea tasting and explore the herbs growing on the Bronx River Foodway. All ages welcome! Bring your family and friends to connect with meditation and locally grown herbs. If called please bring a journal to take notes and\/or write down your thoughts. Note: This is one of this year's Community Grant workshops as well as a part of a larger City of Water Day Celebration supported by the Bronx River Alliance.
address: Bronx River House in Starlight Park

description: Bloom to the Beat is an interactive outdoor workshop with Zinzi Gonzalez where participants explore the Bronx River through sound, storytelling, and floral design. Guests will listen to curated vinyl-inspired playlists rooted in Bronx and NYC music culture while creating their own small floral arrangements inspired by the river's natural elements-growth, flow, and resilience.
address: 1490 Sheridan Blvd

name: City of Water Day: Salt Marsh Exploration
description: Join the Rangers for a special City of Water Day celebration with a guided walk along the Muscota Marsh. Discover the area's biodiversity as the Rangers highlight the birds, mammals, and other wildlife that call this unique habitat home
address: West 218th Street and Indian Road in Inwood Hill Park

name: Cooking on the Farm
description: Experience the flavors of Randall's Island Park Alliance's Urban Farm through this free, interactive cooking demonstration! Join an experienced chef in the Urban Farm Kitchen for an afternoon of farm-to-table fun. After learning about the cultural significance of the recipe and its freshly harvested ingredients, participants will work together to chop, crush, and stir fresh ingredients, and enjoy a small plate of the finished dish. Guests will leave with new knowledge of how to use seasonal produce to cook a healthy and delicious meal. Registration is required. The link will open 2 weeks before the workshop. Location: Urban Farm For inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.
address: Urban Farm in Randall's Island Park

name: From Plant to Paper with Sana Ashraf
description: Join us on the Bronx River with Sana Ashraf for plant-based paper making! While paper products today are largely mass produced, the techniques and guiding methodology are still rooted in its handmade origins. As such, this is a craft that can be easily adapted to a variety of materials, without investing in expensive equipment, and with a little creativity allows us to utilize and upcycle materials available in the plant world, our recycling bins, and even kitchen scraps intended for the compost pile. The session will involve a guided gathering of plant materials* from Concrete Plant Park, which will then be used to implement the process of plant based paper making. *The bulk of this will be focused on plants whose removal benefits the ongoing weeding and other stewardship works. Note: This is one of this year's Community Grant workshops.
address: Bronx River House in Starlight Park

name: Ice Cream Flavors of the Diaspora
description: Modern ice cream production and the plants that create some of our favorite flavors are possible thanks to the brilliance of Black and Indigenous people. During this session we'll learn about the contributions of these people alongside the stories of plants like vanilla, sorghum, sorrel, and blueberries as we create ice creams infused with their flavors. If you want to develop your ice cream making skills and plant knowledge you won't wanna miss this session! NYC Parks GreenThumb hosts this event. We are proud to be the nation's most extensive urban community gardening program, sustaining over 550 gardens throughout New York City. With GreenThumb's support, community gardens are centers of environmental justice, ensuring that our city is fairer, safer, and greener for all. GreenThumb's events are free and open to the public.
address: Tranquility Farm

name: It's My Park at Hunter's Point South Park

address: Hunter's Point South Park

name: Paddle and Pick Up at Starlight Park
description: Join the Bronx River Alliance for a Paddle and Pick Up as part of our City of Water Day Celebration! This guided canoeing river clean up will allow participants to enjoy the river and protect local wildlife at the same time! Participants will be given gloves, grabbers, and trash bags or buckets, to safely remove floatable litter from the Bronx River. Minors above the age of 10 are welcome to participate, but MUST be accompanied by an adult. All minors must have an adult in their boat, and have a waiver signed by a legal guardian. Previous canoeing experience is strongly recommended for this activity. Advanced registration is required to participate.
address: Bronx River House in Starlight Park

name: Park Open Studio
description: Led by a local teaching artist, this drop-in class invites participants to explore creativity through hands-on artmaking under the shade of Scrabble Plaza's trees. Past mediums have included plein-air drawing, printmaking, cyanotypes, and more. All materials are provided by the Washington Square Park Conservancy.
address: Northwest Corner Seating Area in Washington Square Park

Note: This is one of this year's Community Grant workshops.

Campanella, Historian-in-Residence of the NYC Parks Department, author of Brooklyn: The Once and Future City, will be talking about the history of Marine Park. Sponsored by the Salt Marsh Alliance.

Each tour lasts approximately 45 minutes. Drop-in event; no registration required. Today's tour features current exhibitions. Questions? Please email us at information@wavehill.org or call 718.549.3200 x251. Glyndor Gallery is wheelchair-accessible. There is an accessible, ground-level entrance at the front of the building with an elevator that provides access to the gallery level. Participants can stop by for one or more sessions, but kids who stay for the whole program will get the most benefit from it. Occasionally a Summer Sports Experience program may be cancelled due to harsh weather or other conditions. Please note: We recommend that guardians accompany children participating in this program.
